meta.title: Malaysia
meta.destination: Kuala Lumpur · Cameron Highlands · Penang · Langkawi
meta.subtitle: KL → Cameron Highlands → Penang → Langkawi

return.label: Return
return.detail: Thu 1 Jan · OD166 · Batik Air Malaysia

acts.tx: Depart Dhaka DAC — BS315, US-Bangla Airlines. MYR 2,055/person.
Arrive Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KUL). Clear immigration + bags.
Grab to Kingston Hotel 6 @ Changkat Bukit Bintang. ~45 min from KUL, MYR 50–70.
Check in: Kingston Hotel 6 @ Changkat Bukit Bintang, KL.
Batu Caves — iconic Hindu temple complex carved into limestone. Free entry to main cave. 272 rainbow-painted steps. ~1.5 hrs. Train from KL Sentral, MYR 2.60/person.
KLCC Park — 50-acre park at the foot of the Petronas Twin Towers. Lake, jogging path, fountains, and the twin towers lit up at dusk. MYR 30 (for 2).
Museum of Illusions Kuala Lumpur — interactive optical illusion galleries, Vortex Tunnel, Ames Room. MYR 49/person.
Dinner at Damascus Bukit Bintang — Middle Eastern flavors a short walk from hotel. MYR 130 (for 2).
Explore Changkat Bukit Bintang — the hotel's street is KL's main bar and dining strip. Rooftop bars, live music.
Breakfast near hotel before heading out. MYR 10–15/person.
Bus: KL Sentral → Genting Awana Bus Terminal. MYR 22.60/person. ~1 hr. Buy tickets at KL Sentral bus counter.
Awana SkyWay — 3.4 km gondola up to SkyAvenue. MYR 22/person return. Sweeping highland views over the rainforest canopy.
Genting Highlands Premium Outlets — luxury and mid-range brands at outlet prices. Free entry. Cool 1,800 m highland air.
SkyAvenue — indoor entertainment hub at the summit: arcade games, theme rides, dining, cinema. Free entry; pay per ride/activity.
Return bus: Genting Awana → KL Sentral. MYR 22.60/person.
Dinner at Jalan Alor Food Street — KL's most famous outdoor hawker street. Char kway teow, satay, seafood. MYR 50–100/person.
Early breakfast. Head to Terminal Bersepadu Selatan (TBS) — 20 min by Grab from hotel.
Bus: TBS → Melaka Sentral. MYR 34.80/person. ~2 hrs. Frequent departures.
Dutch Square (Red Square) — Stadthuys, Christ Church, and Fountain Square. Iconic red colonial architecture. Free.
St Paul's Hill + Church of Saint Paul — ruined 16th-century Portuguese church with city views. Free.
Lunch in Melaka — chicken rice balls, Nyonya cuisine. MYR 20–40/person.
A Famosa — Portuguese fort remnant from 1511. Historic gateway. Free.
Muzium Istana Kesultanan Melaka — Royal Palace Museum. MYR 2/person. Traditional Malay royal artifacts.
Return bus: Melaka Sentral → TBS. MYR 34.80/person. ~2 hrs.
Back in KL. Drinks and dinner on Changkat Bukit Bintang — rooftop bars, outdoor terrace restaurants, lively Christmas night crowd.
Early breakfast + check out of Kingston Hotel 6.
Bus: TBS → Tanah Rata, Cameron Highlands. MYR 111.88/person (for 2). ~4 hrs through winding highland roads.
Arrive Tanah Rata. Check in: Melur Inn Hotel, Cameron Highlands.
Lunch in Tanah Rata town. Steam boat or roti canai. MYR 15–30/person.
BOH Tea Centre, Sungei Palas Garden — iconic hilltop tea plantation. Free. Overlooking endless rows of tea bushes. Tea and scones at the café.
Cameron Highlands Butterfly Farm — tropical species in a walk-through garden. MYR 8/person.
Sam Poh Temple — ornate Chinese Buddhist temple on a hilltop above Brinchang. Free. Sweeping highland panorama.
Strawberry Farm — pick your own strawberries. MYR 10–15/person. Local strawberry jam, cream, and ice cream available.
Dinner in Tanah Rata. Steamboat hotpot — perfect for the cool highland air. MYR 25–40/person.
Breakfast + check out of Melur Inn Hotel.
Bus: Tanah Rata → Terminal Bas Sungai Nibong, Penang. MYR 90.40/person. ~3.5 hrs through lush jungle and coastal lowlands.
Grab from Sungai Nibong to Rare Heritage House, George Town. ~20 mins.
Check in: Rare Heritage House, Penang.
George Town street art walk — Penang's famous wall murals and iron rod sculptures scattered through the UNESCO heritage streets. Free. Pick up a map from hotel.
Clan Jetties of Penang — Chinese clan villages built on stilts over the water. Chew Jetty is the largest. Free.
George Town Heritage Zone evening stroll — colonial shophouses, Little India, Armenian Street. Free.
Dinner — hawker stalls in George Town. Penang char kway teow, asam laksa, rojak. MYR 20–40/person.
Breakfast at a kopitiam — traditional Penang white coffee, kaya toast, soft-boiled eggs. MYR 10–15/person.
Penang Hill — funicular railway to 830 m summit. MYR 30/person return. Colonial bungalows, Owl Museum, panoramic views of George Town and the strait.
Kek Lok Si Temple — largest Buddhist temple in Malaysia. 7-storey pagoda, bronze Kuan Yin statue. Free (lift to statue: MYR 2). ~1.5 hrs.
Lunch at Gurney Drive Hawker Centre — open-air hawker stalls on the waterfront. Hokkien mee, cendol, grilled corn. MYR 15–30/person.
Cheong Fatt Tze — The Blue Mansion. MYR 17/person guided tour. Stunning indigo-blue 1880s Chinese Baroque mansion. Only 3 tours/day — book ahead.
Final George Town wander — sunset over the colonial waterfront, Armenian Street cafés.
Last dinner in Penang — assam laksa or nasi kandar. MYR 20–40/person.
Breakfast + check out of Rare Heritage House.
Grab to Penang International Airport (PEN).
Flight FY4672, Firefly: PEN → Langkawi LGK. ~30 min. MYR 386/person.
Car rental pickup at Langkawi Airport — LangkawiBook.my. MYR 240 total. Essential for exploring the island.
Check in: Destini Akef Villa, Langkawi.
Langkawi Skybridge Cable Car — gondola to 700 m peak, walk the curved pedestrian bridge over the jungle canopy. MYR 55/person return.
Pantai Cenang — Langkawi's most popular beach. White sand, calm water, beach bars. Free.
Dream Forest Langkawi — magical illuminated forest art installation after dark. MYR 38/person.
Dinner at Pantai Cenang strip — fresh grilled fish, Tom yum, mango sticky rice. MYR 30–60/person.
Breakfast at villa or nearby café.
Mangrove Tour — boat through Kilim Geoforest Park mangroves. Bat caves, fish farm, eagle feeding. ~3 hrs. MYR 80–120/person. Book via hotel.
Lunch at a fish farm on the water — the tour often includes a seafood stop. MYR 20–40.
Island Hopping — Alanona speedboat tour to Pulau Dayang Bunting (Pregnant Maiden Lake), Pulau Singa Besar, and Pulau Beras Basah. MYR 35–50/person. ~3 hrs.
Eagle Square (Dataran Lang) — 12-metre bronze eagle sculpture, Langkawi's icon. Free. Sunset views over the strait.
Dinner — seafood barbecue by the beach. MYR 40–80/person.
Cenang Mall, Langkawi — duty-free shopping: chocolates, alcohol, cosmetics. No GST on Langkawi.
Lunch at beachfront café. MYR 30–50/person.
Alun-Alun Spa, Pantai Cenang — traditional Malay massage and treatments. MYR 80–150/person. Book ahead.
Sunset at Pantai Cenang — one of the best sunsets in Malaysia. Free.
New Year's Eve dinner at Yellow Beach Cafe — waterfront setting, fresh seafood, festive atmosphere. MYR 60–120/person.
New Year's Eve countdown on the beach. Fireworks over the Andaman Sea. Return car tonight and arrange morning transfer.
Check out of Destini Akef Villa. Transfer to Langkawi Airport (LGK) for connecting flight to KUL.
Flight: Langkawi LGK → Kuala Lumpur KUL (AirAsia or Firefly, ~1 hr). Book separately to connect to return flight.
Arrive KUL. Check-in + security. Batik Air OD166, Terminal 2.
Flight OD166, Batik Air Malaysia: KUL → Dhaka DAC. MYR 2,077/person.
Land Dhaka DAC — Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport.

budget.note: DAC→KUL BS315 MYR 2,055 · PEN→LGK FY4672 MYR 386 · KUL→DAC OD166 MYR 2,077
Kingston Hotel 6 MYR 307 (3 nts) · Melur Inn MYR 197 (1 nt) · Rare Heritage House MYR 313 (2 nts) · Destini Akef Villa MYR 416 (3 nts)
KL↔Genting 45.2 · TBS↔Melaka 69.6 · TBS→Cameron 111.88 · Tanah Rata→Penang 90.4 · Car rental Langkawi 240
Damascus 130 · KLCC Park 30 · hawker meals + entry fees + tours + Langkawi activities over 10 days

notes.title: Currency
notes.text: 1 USD ≈ MYR 4.45 · 1 BDT ≈ MYR 0.037 (approx, Dec 2025). Langkawi is duty-free — alcohol, chocolates, and cosmetics are significantly cheaper.

notes.title: Transport
notes.text: Use Grab for city rides in KL and Penang. Car rental is essential in Langkawi — public transport is limited. Book buses from TBS (Terminal Bersepadu Selatan) via Easybook or at the counter.

notes.title: Airports
notes.text: Jan 1 departure is from Kuala Lumpur KUL (not Langkawi). Book a Langkawi→KUL connecting flight separately and allow at least 3 hrs before the KUL→DAC departure.

notes.title: Dress Code
notes.text: Cover shoulders and knees at Sam Poh Temple, Kek Lok Si, and Batu Caves. Modest dress appreciated at all religious sites.

notes.title: Cash
notes.text: Carry MYR cash for hawker centres, temples, and rural stops. ATMs widely available in KL, Penang, and Langkawi town. Grab requires a card linked to the app.
